feature: UX Improvements - onboarding split, settings nav, edit profile expand

prompts.description: Split onboarding entry into two clear paths: Quick Start vs Precise Setup
prompts.prompt:
In components/onboarding.tsx, improve the onboarding entry UX. The file already has a 'quick-estimate' step and a 'body' step as separate entry points (firstStep state). Make the following changes:
1. When the user first lands on onboarding (step === 'quick-estimate' and firstStep === 'quick-estimate'), show a clean two-option card UI at the top: 'Quick Start (2 min)' and 'Precise Setup (5 min)'. Quick Start maps to the existing quick-estimate flow. Precise Setup jumps directly to the 'body' step (setFirstStep('body'), setStep('body')).
2. Add a short subtitle under each option: Quick Start = 'Estimate your body type', Precise Setup = 'Enter exact measurements'.
3. Keep all existing logic intact — only add the two-option entry UI above or replacing the current first-render of the quick-estimate step. Do not change any step logic, validation, or data flow.
4. Use existing Tailwind classes and the app's color tokens (primary, muted, card, etc). No hardcoded hex values.

prompts.description: Add Back to Plan button and wrap Start Over in confirmation dialog
prompts.prompt:
In components/settings-screen.tsx, make two navigation improvements:
1. At the very top of the SettingsScreen component (above all sections), add a visible 'Back to Plan' button that calls setCurrentStep(2). Use an arrow-left icon from lucide-react (ChevronLeft or ArrowLeft). Style it as a small ghost/text button aligned left.
2. Find the existing 'bridge-back-link' button (onClick setCurrentStep(0)) — change its label to 'Reset & Start Over' and wrap it in an AlertDialog confirmation: title='Start Over?', description='This will clear your current meal plan and profile. Are you sure?', confirm button='Yes, start over' (destructive variant), cancel='Cancel'. Only call setCurrentStep(0) after confirmation.
3. Do not change any other logic. Use the existing shadcn/ui AlertDialog component already in the project.

prompts.description: Add diet type and meals-per-day fields to Edit Profile in settings
prompts.prompt:
In components/settings-screen.tsx, expand the Edit Profile section to include two additional editable fields:
1. First, read the file to find the exact field names for diet type and meals per day in the Zustand store (check useMealStore types). Then add a Diet Type select/dropdown using the same diet options already defined in the onboarding flow (check components/onboarding.tsx for the diet options array).
2. Add a Meals Per Day control — either a number input or three buttons (3, 4, 5) — mapped to the correct store field.
3. Place these fields within the existing Edit Profile form after the body/activity fields. On save, call setUserProfile and calculateMacros the same way other fields do.
4. Do not add new store fields — only use fields that already exist.
